seoTitle: South Kununoppin vs South Lake — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between South Kununoppin and South Lake. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: South Kununoppin vs South Lake: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Kununoppin and South Lake.
heading: South Kununoppin vs South Lake — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Kununoppin, Western Australia and South Lake, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Kununoppin has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to South Lake (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Kununoppin or South Lake safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Kununoppin has a flood risk score of 45/100, while South Lake scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Kununoppin scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. South Lake scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Kununoppin or South Lake?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Kununoppin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
